overview.short: Irshalgad is a fortress located between Matheran and Panvel in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Irshalgad is a fortress located between Matheran and Panvel in Maharashtra, India. It is a sister fort to Prabalgad. The area of the fort is not large but there are several water cisterns cut from the rock. The nearest village is Irshalwadi.

nearestAirport.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Morbe Dam
topPlaces.description: Morbe Dam is a gravity dam on the Dhavari river near Khalapur, Raigad district in the state of Maharashtra, India. The Morbe lake is the main water source for the city of Navi Mumbai.

topPlaces.name: Prabalgad
topPlaces.description: Prabalgad (also known as Muranjan, Pradhangad or Prabalmachi) is a fort located between Matheran and Panvel and comes under the Raigad District in the state of Maharashtra, India. The Prabalgad Fort stands at an elevation of 2,300 feet (700 m) in the Western Ghats.

topPlaces.name: Kalavantin Durg
topPlaces.description: Kalavantin Durg is a 2,250 ft (686 m) high summit located in the Western Ghats, near the Prabalgad fort in the Raigad district of Maharashtra, India. It is also known as Kelve Teen, Kalavantinicha Sulka, or Kalavantin Pinnacle; alternative transliterations include Kalawantin, Kalavati, and Kalavanti.

topPlaces.name: Mohpada
topPlaces.description: Mohopada, alias Wasambe, is a census town in Khalapur Taluka, Raigad district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Matheran
topPlaces.description: Matheran (Pronunciation: [maːt̪ʰeɾaːn]) is a partially automobile free hill station and a municipal council in the Karjat taluka of the Raigad district located in the Indian state of Maharashtra. Matheran is part of the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, and one of the smallest hill stations in India.

topPlaces.name: Ambivali Tarf Tungaratan
topPlaces.description: Ambivali Tarf Tungaratan is a census town in Raigad district in the state of Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Ajivali
topPlaces.description: Ajivali is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 469 ha (1,159 acres).

topPlaces.name: Bhatan
topPlaces.description: Bhatan is a village located in Raigad district, Konkan division, in the state of Maharashtra, India. It is 9 km from the Mumbai–Pune Expressway, 10 km away from sub-district headquarter Panvel and 60 km away from district headquarter Alibag.

items.note: Morbe Dam is a gravity dam on the Dhavari river near Khalapur, Raigad district in the state of Maharashtra, India. The Morbe lake is the main water source for the city of Navi Mumbai.

Prabalgad (also known as Muranjan, Pradhangad or Prabalmachi) is a fort located between Matheran and Panvel and comes under the Raigad District in the state of Maharashtra, India. The Prabalgad Fort stands at an elevation of 2,300 feet (700 m) in the Western Ghats.

Kalavantin Durg is a 2,250 ft (686 m) high summit located in the Western Ghats, near the Prabalgad fort in the Raigad district of Maharashtra, India. It is also known as Kelve Teen, Kalavantinicha Sulka, or Kalavantin Pinnacle; alternative transliterations include Kalawantin, Kalavati, and Kalavanti.
Mohopada, alias Wasambe, is a census town in Khalapur Taluka, Raigad district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

Matheran (Pronunciation: [maːt̪ʰeɾaːn]) is a partially automobile free hill station and a municipal council in the Karjat taluka of the Raigad district located in the Indian state of Maharashtra. Matheran is part of the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, and one of the smallest hill stations in India.
Ambivali Tarf Tungaratan is a census town in Raigad district in the state of Maharashtra, India.
Ajivali is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 469 ha (1,159 acres).

Bhatan is a village located in Raigad district, Konkan division, in the state of Maharashtra, India. It is 9 km from the Mumbai–Pune Expressway, 10 km away from sub-district headquarter Panvel and 60 km away from district headquarter Alibag.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Irshalgad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Irshalgad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (42 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (36 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Irshalgad?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Irshalgad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Irshalgad?
faq.a: Stays in Irshalgad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Irshalgad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Morbe Dam, Prabalgad, Kalavantin Durg, Mohpada, Matheran.
